sourcePath: documents/Sent By Anjan/program_content/🧭 RECOVERY COMPASS 6 DAYS PROGRAM.md

cards.goal: Break autopilot. Make smoking inconvenient and conscious

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: Most people believe quitting fails because of weak willpower.
cards.highlight: If smoking requires effort and awareness, its power drops immediately.

cards.title: Today’s Core Rule (non-negotiable)
instructions: You do not decide whether to smoke today.
You only decide to delay every urge by 10 minutes.
No debate. No negotiation. No “just this once” logic.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 1: Decision (5 Minutes)
instructions: Make the decision once — not repeatedly
Say this out loud or internally :
“I am stopping smoking. I don’t need to feel confident. I don’t need to feel
This is not motivation. This is closure of debate.
You are not deciding forever. You are deciding today.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 2: Environment Reset (critical)
instructions: Keeping one cigarette keeps the habit alive.
This step alone reduces smoking by over 50% for most people because:
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 3: Change One Trigger Routine
instructions: Smoking is attached to routines, not desire.
Pick ONE trigger and break it:
Work break → walk instead of sitting
Do not change everything. One change is enough to disrupt the loop.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 4: The Day 1 Urge Protocol (very Important)
steps: You will feel urges today. That is expected.
When an urge hits, do this EXACTLY:
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Start The 10-minute Timer
instructions: Start the timer. Do nothing else yet.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Grounding (2–3 Minutes)
instructions: Press your feet into the ground
This pulls your brain out of habit memory and into the present moment.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Light Movement
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Important Truth
instructions: Most urges peak and fall within 7–10 minutes even if you do nothing.
Smoking does not remove urges — waiting does.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Day 1 Mental Frame (do Not Skip)
instructions: Today you are not trying to succeed.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: If You Slip On Day 1 (read This Carefully)
instructions: A slip does not mean the day failed.
3. Resume the 10-minute delay on the next urge
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

instructions: This is a mechanical day , not an emotional one.

instructions: “Today, I made smoking conscious instead of automatic.

cards.goal: Remove fear of urges. Learn to stay present while urges pass

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: Most people don’t smoke because they enjoy it. They smoke because urges feel
Urges are not commands. They are sensations that rise and fall.
cards.highlight: When fear drops, urges lose power.

cards.title: Today’s Core Rule (non-negotiable)
instructions: You do not try to remove urges.
You allow them to exist while you delay action.
Trying to “get rid of” urges increases panic. Letting them be reduces urgency.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 1: What An Urge Actually Is
steps: An urge is not nicotine demand.
Urges feel strong because they activate urgency — not because they are
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Step 2: Breathing To Calm The System
steps: When an urge appears, begin 4 – 6 breathing.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds
Exhale slowly through the mouth for 6 seconds
Longer exhale signals safety to the nervous system. This reduces the “do
“I’m calming my body, not fixing the urge.”
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Step 3: Mindful Observation (critical Skill)
instructions: Instead of fighting the urge, observe it.
Does it move or stay still?
Do not judge. Do not analyze. Just notice.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 4: The 10-minute Delay (still Mandatory)
instructions: Every urge is delayed by 10 minutes.
This keeps your nervous system calm.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Day 2 Urge Flow (put This Together)
steps: Let the urge move on its own
Most urges will fade before the timer ends.
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Common Day 2 Fears (normal)
instructions: These thoughts are habit fear , not reality.
Urges always change — even strong ones.

instructions: Today is about learning , not winning.

cards.goal: Stay steady through discomfort. Stop mistaking discomfort for danger

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: By Day 3, your body and mind are adjusting.
cards.highlight: Discomfort is a sign of adjustment, not failure.

cards.title: Today’s Core Rule (non-negotiable)
instructions: You do not escape discomfort today.
You let it pass without using smoking as relief.
Escaping discomfort reinforces the habit. Allowing it weakens it.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 1: Understanding Withdrawal (important)
instructions: Withdrawal is not punishment. It is not danger. It is recalibration.
How to regulate stress without smoking
How to respond without habit shortcuts
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 2: Breathing To Reduce Intensity
instructions: Longer exhales calm the urgency signal.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 3: Progressive Muscle Relaxation (key Tool)
instructions: 1. Tense your hands for 5 seconds → release
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 4: The Emergency Urge Protocol (very Important)
steps: If discomfort or urge feels overwhelming:
“I will not decide anything for 15 minutes.”
Urgency always fades when not acted upon.
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Step 5: Protect Your Energy Today
instructions: Today is not a performance day.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.goal: Detach smoking from daily routines. Shift identity from “quitting” to “not smoking”

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: By Day 4, many urges no longer come from discomfort. They come from routine

cards.title: Today’s Core Rule (non-negotiable)
instructions: You do not negotiate with routine-based urges.
You interrupt them and move on.
No analysis. No debate. No “why is this happening?”
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 1: Identity Shift (most Important Part)
instructions: You are not giving something up. You are living differently today.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 2: Routine Disruption (practical Action)
instructions: Choose at least ONE routine and change it deliberately.
Work breaks → walk instead of sitting
You don’t need many changes. One consistent change weakens the habit loop.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 3: Urge Response Script (use This Verbatim)
steps: When a routine-based urge appears, say internally:
“This doesn’t belong to my day anymore.”
Do not explain. Do not justify. Do not argue.
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Step 4: Replacement Routine (lock One In)
instructions: Pick ONE simple replacement and use it every time.
Consistency trains the brain faster than novelty.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 5: Use Tools Only If Needed
instructions: Some urges may pass without tools
Do not force breathing or grounding unless needed. Let confidence build
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

instructions: When routines change, habits lose their anchor.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.goal: Shift from effort → normal living. Reduce monitoring and over-control

cards.title: Today's Focus
paragraphs: That expectation can actually keep the habit alive.

cards.title: Today’s Core Rule (non-negotiable)
instructions: You live your day and respond only if needed.
No constant checking. No monitoring how you feel. No counting hours.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 1: Recognize The Shift (important)
instructions: Urges that appear and disappear quickly
Moments where smoking doesn’t come to mind
The habit loop is losing relevance.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 2: Reduce Tool Use (intentional)
instructions: Tools still exist — but you don’t use them automatically.
Use delay only if an urge appears
Do not force grounding or relaxation
Confidence grows when tools become optional.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 3: Return To Normal Life
instructions: You are not “in recovery mode” today. You are living.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

cards.title: Step 4: Handle Surprise Urges Calmly
cards.completionMessage: Stay with the practice until your body and mind feel steadier.

cards.title: Step 5: Stop Counting & Comparing
instructions: These keep the habit mentally active.
cards.whyThisWorks: Small consistent actions reinforce stability and reduce automatic, stress-driven reactions.

instructions: These thoughts are normal — and harmless.
Day 6 is not an ending. It’s a handoff to normal life.
